meta.source: docs folder + repository audit + platform implementation progress
meta.purpose: Continuation roadmap after stabilization, orchestration, governance, and enterprise platform expansion

still_pending.title: Real DAISY Ace binary execution with tools_service integration
still_pending.note: production_toolchain.py run_ace() uses hardcoded ['node','ace']; must call tools_service.resolve('ace'), parse JSON report output, and persist results via Q.log_qa_run()

still_pending.title: Real EPUBCheck execution with JAR path resolution
still_pending.note: run_epubcheck() uses hardcoded ['java','epubcheck']; must resolve via tools_service.resolve('epubcheck'), handle JAR invocation, normalize severity levels, persist XML report

still_pending.title: Workflow retry orchestration with backoff and persistent history
still_pending.note: epub_qa.retry_pipeline() only sets a status string; needs: exponential backoff scheduler, retry_count cap, escalation after exhaustion, persistent retry_history table

phase_17_functional_completion.description: Complete enterprise workflow functionality and operational automation.

steps.title: Real DAISY Ace binary execution
requirements: Replace hardcoded ['node','ace'] with tools_service.resolve('ace') lookup
Validate runtime dependencies before execution
Capture and parse JSON QA artifacts from ace --outdir output
Persist execution telemetry via Q.log_qa_run()

steps.title: Real EPUBCheck execution
requirements: Resolve epubcheck JAR via tools_service.resolve('epubcheck') securely
Handle both bare-binary and java -jar invocation paths
Persist XML validation reports
Normalize validation severity levels to SUCCESS/WARNING/FAILURE
Expose structured remediation guidance in output

steps.title: Workflow retry orchestration
requirements: Implement automatic retry scheduling in WorkerRuntime
Add exponential backoff policy (e.g. 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s)
Escalate to NotificationService after retry exhaustion
Persist retry attempt history to a retry_event table or metadata_event

phase_18_performance_optimization.description: Optimize scalability, orchestration throughput, and operational responsiveness.

steps.title: Database indexing and query optimization
requirements: Add indexes for workflow queries (braille_job.priority, lp_ebraille_job.job_type, metadata_event.job_type+job_id)
Optimize provenance history retrieval with covering indexes
Reduce analytics aggregation latency with summary views or materialized counts
Benchmark large queue performance

steps.title: Async workflow execution engine
requirements: Migrate blocking threading.Thread workers to asyncio or concurrent.futures executor
Parallelize independent DAG nodes in workflow_dag.py
Support concurrent QA execution across multiple assets
Implement worker pool size management

steps.title: UI rendering optimization
requirements: Paginate large operational tables (currently unbounded SELECT * queries)
Lazy-load analytics panels and lineage graph
Reduce redundant full-page clears and re-renders on filter changes
Add cached dashboard summary queries with TTL

phase_19_observability_and_metrics.description: Expand operational telemetry and infrastructure observability.

steps.title: Prometheus metrics exporter
requirements: Add prometheus_client dependency and /metrics NiceGUI route
Expose workflow queue depth, completed count, and failure rate
Track SLA breach frequency from SLAMonitoringService
Track worker execution latency from WorkerRuntime
Publish queue throughput metrics

steps.title: Health monitoring endpoints
requirements: Add /health NiceGUI route returning JSON status
Database connectivity check via get_conn()
Queue health verification (pending job count)
Toolchain readiness checks via tools_service.status()
Worker availability reporting from WorkerRuntime

steps.title: Operational anomaly detection
requirements: Detect abnormal queue growth (configurable threshold)
Detect repeated workflow failures (N failures in window)
Detect SLA degradation trends over rolling time window
Trigger automated escalations via NotificationService

phase_20_deployment_and_scaling.description: Prepare the platform for production deployment and horizontal scaling.

steps.title: Containerized deployment support
requirements: Dockerfile with uv-based Python 3.11 image
docker-compose.yml with volume mounts for DB, artifacts, backups, job_files
Environment variable configuration management via .env
Health check instruction in Dockerfile using /health endpoint

steps.title: Secrets and credential management
requirements: Replace plaintext JSON storage in secrets/auth.json with encrypted storage (e.g. cryptography.fernet)
Environment-based credential injection as fallback to file store
Secret rotation support with versioned key slots
Audit visibility for secret access events via AuditLogService

steps.title: Horizontal worker scaling
requirements: Distributed worker registration with heartbeat tracking
Lease expiry requeue: reclaim stale leases when worker dies
Cross-node workflow ownership via worker_id in queue records
Scalable orchestration telemetry (per-worker metrics)
